NPC_._comment: AWPB — reverse-engineered: 4 bytes = FormID 0x008BA6D3 in test record ATX_CAMPPets_Actor_RadHog_Standard. Absent from TES5Edit FO76 reference.
NPC_.name: AWPB

NPC_._comment: NPC_ conditions block (CITC count + CTDA array). CTDA structure is reference-grounded (same 32-byte layout as PERK/QUST conditions).
NPC_.name: Conditions

GMRW._comment: XALG — live-game drift subrecord absent from TES5Edit GMRW definition; expanded from wbXALG (u64 legendary flags).

LVLI._comment: LVLD (Chance None) is wbBelowVersion(174) in TES5Edit — live data (fv≥174) still carries an empty LVLD subrecord not covered by the below_version:174 schema member.
LVLI.name: Chance None (unused)

LVLN._comment: Same as LVLI — empty LVLD subrecord at fv≥174.
LVLN.name: Chance None (unused)

LVPC._comment: Same as LVLI — empty LVLD subrecord at fv≥174.
LVPC.name: Chance None (unused)

LVLP._comment: Same as LVLI — empty LVLD subrecord at fv≥174.
LVLP.name: Chance None (unused)

RESO._comment: NAM5 — reverse-engineered: 1 byte in test record ATX_Resource_TeaWizard_resource. Absent from TES5Edit RESO definition.
RESO.name: NAM5

STAT._comment: SNAM — live-game drift (20260702 snapshot, ATX_/SCORE_S26_ statics); absent from TES5Edit STAT. Verified FormID → SNDR on 0x008D2753; matches MSTT/ASPC/SCOL 'Looping Sound' wbFormIDCk(SNAM,[SNDR]) precedent (wbDefinitionsFO76.pas:9223).
STAT.name: Looping Sound

STAT._comment: ANLD — live-game drift; sig is '{ New To Fallout 76 }' in xEdit and only defined as wbUnknown(ANLD) on MSTT (wbDefinitionsFO76.pas:9224). 8 zero bytes in all observed records; kept as unknown/hex like MSTT's.
STAT.name: Unknown

REFR._comment: MCND — live-game drift; absent from TES5Edit REFR in FO76, FO4, and SF1 (checked all three wbDefinitions*.pas). Seen only on the map-marker REFR HighwayTownMapMarker (0x007E808C), which carries 2 occurrences of 4 zero bytes. Kept as unknown/hex, wrapped in an rarray (matching NOCM's wbRArray('Unknown Datas', wbUnknown(DATA)) precedent, wbDefinitionsFO76.pas:15884) since a plain (non-array) 'unknown' member only consumes one of the 2 instances from the shared by_sig queue, leaving the second as _unmapped.
REFR.name: Unknown MCND

PERK._comment: wbPerkEffect is a HARD_RAW_VAR (runtime Pascal decider) — extractor cannot statically resolve the Effect element. This hand-authored rstruct has been validated against real ESM data. All other PERK members (EDID, DURL, VMAD, FULL, DESC, ICON, Conditions, Data, SNAM, PRFS, NNAM, FNAM, PRFI, PFAC) now come from native extraction — see parity-exceptions.json PERK[1:Effects][elem].

PCRD._comment: xEdit names this struct 'Unknown' verbatim (wbDefinitionsFO76.pas: wbStruct(DATA, 'Unknown', [...])) even though every field is fully decoded — Value/Min Level/Special/Race Restriction. Renamed here so it stops reading as an undecoded blob. Race Restriction (offset 6) is the race-eligibility byte: 0=None, 1=Human, 2=Ghoul.

WEAP._comment: xEdit still names this DNAM field 'Min Power Per Shot' (wbDefinitionsFO76.pas: wbFromVersion(105, wbFloat('Min Power Per Shot'))), but the engine repurposed it to the charge-weapon full-power-shot bonus (~2025) — same field, opposite name. Renamed 2026-07-14 to 'Max Power Per Shot' to fix the min/max direction, then renamed again 2026-07-15 to 'Full Power Damage Mult': 'Max' still implied a variable stat with an unstated minimum, when this is really a fixed bonus applied only at full charge. The new name pairs with the sibling field 'Full Power Seconds' (time to reach full charge) and matches the '...DamageMult' suffix used by real sibling OMOD properties (OutOfRangeDamageMult, CriticalDamageMult) — see _WEAPON_PROPERTIES in extract.py and the mechanics-kb 'Property-name errata' note.

QUST._comment: xEdit leaves this as wbByteArray(QTFS, 'QTFS - Unknown 2 bytes', 2) — undecoded. Observed 0xffff ('no limit') -> 50 on the seasonal-to-repeatable quest conversion SDOW_SQ01_Graves_Repeatable (0x008F1665) in snapshot 20260710, which reads cleanly as a little-endian u16 repeat limit. Decoding the 2 bytes as one u16 is the natural low-level mapping; name hedged with '?' since the repeat-limit semantic is inferred, not confirmed. See mechanics-kb.md 'Known schema gaps'.

RACE._comment: Body Data Male Parts — stop_before bounds the array to Male's own single real 'Part' (anchored on INDX, a signature shared globally with Body Data Female's Parts and Head Datas' Male/Female Head Parts — by_sig is one FIFO queue per signature for the whole record, not per-array). Without a boundary, this unbounded-count rarray keeps popping INDX/MODL/MODT off the shared queue past Male's own part and swallows Female Body Data's part too. FNAM (Female Data Marker, opens the next section) is the sig that immediately follows in document order; GNAM (RACE's own 'Body Part Data', the next record-level member after Body Data closes) is a second boundary for the case where Female Body Data has zero parts. Needs record_patches rather than the extractor's automatic stop_before pass because that pass explicitly excludes rstruct-element rarrays (an optional leading anchor risks a false halt) — see _annotate_stop_before in extract.py.

Body Data Female Parts — same INDX FIFO-sharing hazard as Male's Parts above, mirrored: bounds Female's array to stop at GNAM (the next record-level member after Body Data closes) so it can't run on past Body Data's own end.

Head Datas Male Head Parts — same INDX FIFO-sharing hazard: 'Male Head Parts' has no upper bound, so without a boundary it swallows Female Head Data's (INDX HEAD) pairs too (and, pre-Task-2-extractor-fix, Body Data's INDX subrecords as well, since a sigless wbUnused(INDX, 0) never consumed its subrecord from by_sig). RPRM (Male Race Presets) immediately follows the array in document order; AHCM/FTSM/DFTM/TTGP/NAM0 (the remainder of Male Head Data, and the second NAM0 'Head Data Marker' that opens Female Head Data) are further defense for RACE records whose Male section has fewer than the usual 6 entries.

Head Datas Female Head Parts — mirrors Male Head Parts above. RPRF (Female Race Presets) immediately follows in document order; AHCF/FTSF/DFTF/TTGP are the remainder of Female Head Data — the last repeated section in RACE's document order, so no further NAM0-style boundary is needed after it.
